channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Garmin is getting into events, starting with marathons in Ohio and Arizona
item.description: Garmin has announced a new Marathon Series events franchise for runners, here's what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: What is Amazon's Project Kuiper? The Starlink rival explained ahead of tomorrow's big lift-off
item.description: Amazon is launching a low-cost satellite internet service and you might be able to access it later this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11 users are getting fixes for some longstanding bugs in 24H2 today, including the cure for a seriously annoying File Explorer glitch
item.description: Microsoft is finally resolving some persistent bugs in Windows 11 24H2 with its April update which should be imminent now.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Redbox goes up for auction following a wild bankruptcy saga
item.description: Redbox is getting ready for one final sale. The defunct DVD rental chain’s assets, and those of its corporate siblings Crackle and Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, are being auctioned off in New York later this month, according to a court filing published Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke: AI is now a ‘fundamental expectation’ for employees
item.description: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke shared an internal memo on X Monday that stressed the importance of using AI effectively in daily tasks. In fact, he wrote, using AI is now a “fundamental expectation” of Shopify employees.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing translations of its products after its contract with an artificial intelligence company was allowed to lapse.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration
item.description: Max Everett, a former Energy Department CIO, offers some advice for how new agency technology leaders can deal with some basic challenges. The post Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: CIOs brace for tariff impacts on tech industry and their businesses
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s escalating tariff war is expected to upend the IT investment plans of global CIOs and greatly impact all sectors of the IT industry in the coming months, slowing adoption of AI and damaging long-established supply chains, perhaps for good.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This Ryzen-powered NAS is barely bigger than a shoe box and can hold 11 SSDs and HDDs, delivering more than 500TB of storage
item.description: The Aoostar WTR Max is a powerhouse in a compact form, delivering Ryzen power while fitting into a NAS barely bigger than a shoebox.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Federal Appeals Panel Pauses Order Restricting Access to Sensitive Data
item.description: A federal appeals panel on Monday paused an order curtailing the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, reopening the floodgates for the cost-cutting advisory board.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: De ChatGPT a Chief AI Officer: el cambio en la era de los agentes digitales
item.description: Andrés Pazos, con una visión estratégica y pedagógica, abordó los principales retos y oportunidades que plantea la IA en múltiples sectores, desde la salud hasta el marketing, sin evitar asuntos espinosos como su elevado gasto energético o el miedo a utilizarla por el riesgo de infringir los derecho
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Silicon Valley isn’t pushing back on Trump’s immigration crackdown. It should be
item.description: President Donald Trump has just expanded the social media surveillance system for immigrants. Starting in May, the United States will begin collecting the social media handles of all permanent resident applicants.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Why iPhone-as-a-service may make sense as tariffs bite Apple
item.description: It might be time for Apple-as-a-service as the company looks to plot a cunning course through the post-globalist freeze — and offering its products as a service could help it do just that. Apple had been expected to introduce just such a service.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google NotebookLM cheat sheet: Get fast insights into your docs
item.description: When Google first launched the NotebookLM beta in late 2023, the company billed it as an experimental note-taking app with a generative AI assistant built in. You can still use it to create notes, but the app has evolved into something much more novel and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP rep says Russia and China are 'laughing at us' after NSA director firing
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.), a moderate Republican, slammed President Trump’s decision to fire the director of the National Security Agency (NSA) and his top deputy after demands from far-right influencer Laura Loomer, saying Sunday that China and Russia are “laughing at us” after that decision.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft celebrates 50 years of adapting to change
item.description: On April 4, 1975, two childhood friends, Paul Allen and Bill Gates, started what is today one of the world’s leading technology companies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan begins to lay out technology roadmap
item.description: He’s only been on the job for two weeks, but newly appointed Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan gave a major speech at a partner conference this week where he said the ailing company needs to get its act together, and he doesn’t want customers to hold back on their criticism.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5
